texto: But that's always a certain way to recognise a facist: when he's more powerful he kills everything that's different from him, he uses only brute force while law breaks like glass under his boots. And then, when he loses and when he's weak, he invokes the law and tolerance of differences. All of a sudden, he knows by heart every single human rights convention he broke so many times before.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Andrej Nikolaidis: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “But that's always a certain way to recognise a facist: when he's more powerful he kills everythin…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, equality, facism pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de facism e human-rights.
